center.name: 温带针叶林
center.summary: 温带针叶林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联6种鸟类，包括小太平鸟、台湾戴菊、Winter wren等6种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: A.|last8=Machell|first8=E.|year=1999|title=Hermit Thrush and Black-throated Gray Warbler, new for Cuba, and other significant bird records from Cayo Coco and vicinity, Ciego de Avila Province, Cuba, 1995–1997|journal=Florida Field Naturalist|volume=27|issue=2|pages=37–51|url=http://www.fosbirds.org/sites/default/files/FFNs/FFNv27n2p37-51Wallace.pdf|access-date=2014-07-18|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150924015001/http://www.fosbirds.org/sites/default/files/FFNs/FFNv27n2p37-51Wallace.pdf|archive-date=2015-09-24}} The black-throated gray warbler breeds in open coniferous and mixed forest with a brushy understory, in dry open oak forests, and in chaparral and other scrubland. It is particularly associated with pinyon pines, junipers, and oaks. It migrates to the south late in the fall, returning north in mid-spring. While migrating, it forages in any woodland or scrub it passes through

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Harris's sparrow breeds in stunted coniferous forests and adjacent scrubs, especially areas of the grand boreal forests where stands of spruce abut mossy bogs

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: troglodytes having priority), until they were split in 2010. It breeds in coniferous forests from British Columbia to the Atlantic Ocean

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It usually inhabits evergreen trees in coniferous forests over 2,000 m above sea level, though it is commonest above 2,500 m and ranges upward to 3,700 m

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|left|Two Japanese waxwings feeding on berries The Japanese waxwing breeds in the dense coniferous forest in the Russian Far East (the Amur basin, northern Primorsky Krai, and Sakhalin) and northeasternmost China (Manchuria, Heilongjiang province)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in the vast coniferous forests in north-east China, North Korea and south-east Siberia including Sakhalin and Kamchatka
